refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analyzing terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating terrain with relevant site features and attributes
Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational and safety overlays
Produce an abstracted spatial representation supporting integration with planning and execution modules
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a modular worksite model combining terrain data with operational and safety overlays

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and operational data within a single model may risk coupling if not modularized
coupling_check.notes: By modularizing terrain representation and operational overlays, the DP supports independence and clear interfaces, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain data and site feature definitions
Risk that operational policies and safety plans may change frequently, requiring flexible model updates
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the modular worksite model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the modular boundaries within the worksite model to explicitly separate terrain representation from operational and safety overlays to enhance independence and maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the integration and processing of environmental and operational data to create a comprehensive modular worksite model, including terrain data and overlays for operational and safety information.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time terrain and operational data from sensors and vehicles.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety-related alerts and overlays to other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates data exchange with external systems and modules for updating and distributing the worksite model.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the worksite model logically belongs to the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion since most data processing and overlay integration is centralized here.
Low containment; vehicles consume the model but do not create or integrate it, leading to high diffusion if contained here.
Partial use of the model for remote control, but not responsible for model development, causing moderate diffusion.
Uses safety overlays but does not integrate terrain or operational data fully, resulting in moderate containment and diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP because it centralizes the integration of terrain, operational, and safety data into a modular worksite model, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require robust interface management to prevent diffusion.
If safety overlays require frequent updates from Safety Services, some diffusion may occur across module boundaries.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying the development of a modular worksite model that combines terrain data with operational and safety overlays, directly fulfilling the requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time data feeds may require robust interface management to prevent diffusion.
If safety overlays require frequent updates from Safety Services, some diffusion may occur across module boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment for the DP, centralizing integration and processing of terrain, operational, and safety data. Minimal diffusion is observed within this module, with other modules showing lower containment and higher diffusion scores, supporting the chosen IM boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module provides the integrated worksite model/map to planning and execution functions, enabling validation against acceptance criteria for site features and safety plans.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well-justified, with the module boundary encompassing all necessary data integration and processing responsibilities.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface robustness to manage dependencies on real-time data feeds and minimize diffusion risks.
Establish clear update protocols between Safety Services and NGHE Solution to reduce diffusion related to safety overlay updates.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear logical mapping, high cohesion, and feasible verification. The NGHE Solution module effectively contains the DP with minimal diffusion, and the functional requirement is fully addressed by the current design. Although some diffusion risks exist due to real-time data dependencies and safety overlay updates, these are manageable through interface robustness and update protocols, which are recommended revisions rather than reasons to further decompose. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear, well-contained module boundary is preferable to avoid complexity and potential diffusion from deeper decomposition.

revise.why: Enhance interface robustness to manage dependencies on real-time data feeds and establish clear update protocols between Safety Services and NGHE Solution to minimize diffusion risks and ensure safety-critical integrity.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can be effectively enhanced to handle interface robustness and update protocols.
Verification methods are sufficient to validate the integrated worksite model and safety overlays.
Current DP fully satisfies the FR without need for further decomposition.
open_risks: Potential diffusion from real-time data feed dependencies if interface robustness is not adequately implemented.
Frequent updates to safety overlays may cause diffusion if update protocols are not strictly followed.
evidence_needed: Verification test results confirming integrated model accuracy and safety overlay correctness.
Interface robustness validation under real-time data feed conditions.
Documentation of update protocols and their enforcement between Safety Services and NGHE Solution module.
